ddls:
CREATE TABLE "video_streams" (
  "stream_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each video stream */,
  "duration" INTEGER /* Duration of the video stream in seconds */,
  "size" INTEGER /* Size of the video stream in bytes */,
  "bitrate" INTEGER /* Bitrate of the video stream in bits per second */,
  "width" INTEGER /* Width of the video stream in pixels */,
  "height" INTEGER /* Height of the video stream in pixels */,
  "frame_rate" INTEGER /* Frame rate of the video stream in frames per second */,
  "bitrate_type" TEXT /* Type of bitrate (e.g., constant, variable) */,
  "resolution" TEXT /* Resolution of the video stream */,
  "framerate" INTEGER /* Frame rate of the video stream */,
  "mpeg4" INTEGER /* Indicates if MPEG-4 codec is used (0 or 1) */,
  "vp9" INTEGER /* Indicates if VP9 codec is used (0 or 1) */,
  "hevc" INTEGER /* Indicates if HEVC codec is used (0 or 1) */,
  "percentage" REAL /* Percentage of the video stream's size relative to the total */,
  "content_id" INTEGER /* ID of the video content */,
  "provider_id" INTEGER /* ID of the content provider */,
  "encoding_time" INTEGER /* Time taken to encode the video stream */,
  "encoding_quality" REAL /* Quality score for the encoded video */,
  "streaming_protocol" TEXT /* Streaming protocol used (e.g., HLS, DASH) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("stream_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_video_streams_provider_id FOREIGN KEY ("provider_id") REFERENCES content_providers ("provider_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_video_streams_content_id FOREIGN KEY ("content_id") REFERENCES video_content ("content_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "encoding_settings" (
  "setting_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each encoding setting */,
  "codec" TEXT /* Codec used for encoding (e.g., MPEG-4, VP9, HEVC) */,
  "bitrate" INTEGER /* Bitrate setting in bits per second */,
  "resolution" TEXT /* Resolution setting (e.g., 1280x720) */,
  "frame_rate" INTEGER /* Frame rate setting in frames per second */,
  "profile" TEXT /* Encoding profile (e.g., baseline, main, high) */,
  "preset" TEXT /* Encoding preset (e.g., fast, medium, slow) */,
  "keyframe_interval" INTEGER /* Interval at which keyframes are inserted */,
  "gop_size" INTEGER /* Size of the Group of Pictures (GOP) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("setting_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "stream_performance" (
  "performance_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each performance metric */,
  "stream_id" INTEGER /* ID of the video stream */,
  "bitrate" INTEGER /* Bitrate in bits per second */,
  "resolution" TEXT /* Resolution (e.g., 1280x720) */,
  "frame_rate" INTEGER /* Frame rate in frames per second */,
  "percentage" REAL /* Percentage of the video stream's size relative to the total */,
  "buffering_time" INTEGER /* Total buffering time during the stream */,
  "stall_count" INTEGER /* Number of stalls (buffering events) during the stream */,
  "network_quality" REAL /* Score indicating the quality of the network during the stream */,
  "playback_quality" REAL /* Score indicating the quality of playback */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("performance_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_stream_performance_stream_id FOREIGN KEY ("stream_id") REFERENCES video_streams ("stream_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "stream_access" (
  "access_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each access event */,
  "stream_id" INTEGER /* ID of the video stream being accessed */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user accessing the video stream */,
  "access_time" TEXT /* Time when the video stream was accessed */,
  "access_type" TEXT /* Type of access (e.g., view, download) */,
  "device_id" INTEGER /* ID of the device used */,
  "location" TEXT /* Geographical location of the user */,
  "ip_address" TEXT /* IP address of the user */,
  "user_agent" TEXT /* User agent string of the device */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("access_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_stream_access_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_stream_access_stream_id FOREIGN KEY ("stream_id") REFERENCES video_streams ("stream_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_stream_access_device_id FOREIGN KEY ("device_id") REFERENCES devices ("device_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "users" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user */,
  "user_name" TEXT /* Full name of the user */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the user */,
  "role" TEXT /* Role of the user (e.g., viewer, editor, admin) */,
  "subscription_type" TEXT /* Type of subscription (e.g., free, premium) */,
  "signup_date" TEXT /* Date the user signed up */,
  "last_activity" TEXT /* Date and time of the user's last activity */,
  "preferred_quality" TEXT /* User's preferred video quality (e.g., SD, HD, 4K) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "devices" (
  "device_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each device */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user using the device */,
  "device_type" TEXT /* Type of device (e.g., smartphone, tablet, computer) */,
  "device_model" TEXT /* Model of the device */,
  "operating_system" TEXT /* Operating system of the device */,
  "screen_resolution" TEXT /* Screen resolution of the device */,
  "network_type" TEXT /* Type of network (e.g., Wi-Fi, cellular) */,
  "battery_level" INTEGER /* Battery level of the device during access */,
  "connection_speed" INTEGER /* Speed of the network connection */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("device_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_devices_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "network_conditions" (
  "condition_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each network condition */,
  "access_id" INTEGER /* ID of the access event */,
  "bandwidth" INTEGER /* Bandwidth in bits per second */,
  "latency" INTEGER /* Latency in milliseconds */,
  "packet_loss" INTEGER /* Packet loss percentage */,
  "jitter" INTEGER /* Variation in latency */,
  "round_trip_time" INTEGER /* Round-trip time for network packets */,
  "throughput" INTEGER /* Total throughput of the network */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("condition_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_network_conditions_access_id FOREIGN KEY ("access_id") REFERENCES stream_access ("access_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "predictions" (
  "prediction_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each prediction */,
  "stream_id" INTEGER /* ID of the video stream */,
  "issue_type" TEXT /* Type of issue (e.g., bandwidth congestion, packet loss) */,
  "predicted_time" TEXT /* Predicted time when the issue may occur */,
  "confidence" REAL /* Confidence level of the prediction */,
  "impact" TEXT /* Potential impact of the predicted issue */,
  "recommended_action" TEXT /* Recommended action to mitigate the predicted issue */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("prediction_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_predictions_stream_id FOREIGN KEY ("stream_id") REFERENCES video_streams ("stream_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "ab_tests" (
  "test_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each A/B test */,
  "setting_id" INTEGER /* ID of the encoding setting being tested */,
  "start_time" TEXT /* Start time of the A/B test */,
  "end_time" TEXT /* End time of the A/B test */,
  "result" TEXT /* Result of the A/B test */,
  "test_group" TEXT /* Group to which the test belongs (e.g., A, B) */,
  "sample_size" INTEGER /* Number of users or streams in the test */,
  "test_description" TEXT /* Description of the test */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("test_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_ab_tests_setting_id FOREIGN KEY ("setting_id") REFERENCES encoding_settings ("setting_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "test_results" (
  "result_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each test result */,
  "test_id" INTEGER /* ID of the A/B test */,
  "metric" TEXT /* Performance metric being evaluated (e.g., bitrate, resolution) */,
  "value" INTEGER /* Value of the performance metric */,
  "feedback" TEXT /* User feedback on the A/B test */,
  "test_group" TEXT /* Group to which the result belongs (e.g., A, B) */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user providing the feedback */,
  "device_id" INTEGER /* ID of the device used during the test */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("result_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_test_results_test_id FOREIGN KEY ("test_id") REFERENCES ab_tests ("test_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_test_results_device_id FOREIGN KEY ("device_id") REFERENCES devices ("device_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_test_results_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "user_feedback" (
  "feedback_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each piece of feedback */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user providing the feedback */,
  "stream_id" INTEGER /* ID of the video stream being feedback on */,
  "rating" INTEGER /* Rating of the video streaming experience (1-5) */,
  "comment" TEXT /* Comment from the user */,
  "network_conditions_id" INTEGER /* ID of the network conditions */,
  "device_id" INTEGER /* ID of the device used */,
  "location" TEXT /* Geographical location of the user */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("feedback_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_user_feedback_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_user_feedback_network_conditions_id FOREIGN KEY ("network_conditions_id") REFERENCES network_conditions ("condition_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_user_feedback_stream_id FOREIGN KEY ("stream_id") REFERENCES video_streams ("stream_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_user_feedback_device_id FOREIGN KEY ("device_id") REFERENCES devices ("device_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "content_providers" (
  "provider_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each content provider */,
  "provider_name" TEXT /* Name of the content provider */,
  "contact_email" TEXT /* Contact email for the content provider */,
  "website" TEXT /* Website of the content provider */,
  "phone_number" TEXT /* Phone number of the content provider */,
  "address" TEXT /* Physical address of the content provider */,
  "rating" REAL /* Average rating of the content provider */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("provider_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "video_content" (
  "content_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each piece of video content */,
  "provider_id" INTEGER /* ID of the content provider */,
  "title" TEXT /* Title of the video content */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the video content */,
  "category" TEXT /* Category of the video content (e.g., movie, series, documentary) */,
  "tags" TEXT /* Tags or keywords associated with the video content */,
  "upload_date" TEXT /* Date the video content was uploaded */,
  "view_count" INTEGER /* Number of times the video content has been viewed */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("content_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_video_content_provider_id FOREIGN KEY ("provider_id") REFERENCES content_providers ("provider_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "content_access" (
  "access_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each access event */,
  "content_id" INTEGER /* ID of the video content being accessed */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user accessing the video content */,
  "access_time" TEXT /* Time when the video content was accessed */,
  "access_type" TEXT /* Type of access (e.g., view, download) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("access_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_content_access_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_content_access_content_id FOREIGN KEY ("content_id") REFERENCES video_content ("content_id")
)
